title: Sarga 62
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: The passage moves from “When the sages had spoken thus, Rama, with folded hands, replied to them, asking what Lavana's food and conduct were, and where he resided.” through “Having thus promised those sages of fierce ascetic power, Rama then spoke to all his assembled brothers.” through “Indeed, Ayodhya, though desolate, was formerly protected by the noble Bharata, who kept sorrow in his heart regarding the return of the noble…”

Themes: mind and meditation, virtue and right conduct, knowledge and discernment. The passage moves from “O King, Bharata indeed experienced many sufferings here, formerly residing in Nandigrama, lying on uncomfortable beds.” through “You are brave, learned, and capable in establishing a kingdom.” through “O Kakutstha, accept this consecration prepared by me, which is performed by prominent Brahmins like Vasistha, accompanied by proper rituals and…”

verses.sanskrit: तथोक्ते तानृषीन्रामः प्रत्युवाच कृताञ्जलिः । किमाहारः किमाचारो लवणः क्व च वर्तते ।। 7.62.1 ।।
verses.translation: When the sages had spoken thus, Rama, with folded hands, replied to them, asking what Lavana's food and conduct were, and where he resided.

verses.sanskrit: राघवस्य वचः श्रुत्वा ऋषयः सर्व एव ते । ततो निवेदयामासुर्लवणो ववृधे यथा ।। 7.62.2 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rama's words, all those sages then reported how Lavana had grown in power.

verses.sanskrit: आहारः सर्वसत्त्वानि विशेषेण च तापसाः । आचारो रौद्रता नित्यं वासो मधुवने तथा ।। 7.62.3 ।।
verses.translation: His food consists of all beings, especially ascetics, his conduct is perpetually ferocious, and his dwelling is in the Madhuvana forest.

verses.sanskrit: हत्वा बहुसहस्राणि सिंहव्याघ्रमृगद्विपान् । मानुषांश्चैव कुरुते नित्यमाहारमाह्निकम् ।। 7.62.4 ।।
verses.translation: Having killed many thousands of lions, tigers, deer, elephants, and humans, he makes them his daily meal.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽन्तराणि सत्त्वानि खादते स महाबलः । संहारे समनुप्राप्ते व्यादितास्य इवान्तकः ।। 7.62.5 ।।
verses.translation: Then, that mighty one devours other beings, like Yama with an open mouth at the time of cosmic dissolution.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवो वाक्यमुवाच स महामुनीन् । घातयिष्यामि तद्रक्षो ह्यपगच्छतु वो भयम् ।। 7.62.6 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that, Rama spoke these words to the great sages: "I shall indeed kill that demon; let your fear depart."

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञाय तथा तेषां मुनीनामुग्रतेजसाम् । स भ्रातऽन्सहितान्सर्वानुवाच रघुनन्दनः ।। 7.62.7 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus promised those sages of fierce ascetic power, Rama then spoke to all his assembled brothers.

verses.sanskrit: को हन्ता लवणं वीरः कस्यांशः स विधीयताम् । भरतस्य महाबाहोः शत्रुघ्नस्य च धीमतः ।। 7.62.8 ।।
verses.translation: "Which hero will kill Lavana? To whom among the mighty-armed Bharata and the intelligent Shatrughna should that task be assigned?"

verses.sanskrit: राघवेणैवमुक्तस्तु भरतो वाक्यमब्रवीत् । अहमेनं वधिष्यामि ममांशः स विधीयताम् ।। 7.62.9 ।।
verses.translation: When Rama thus spoke, Bharata replied, "I will kill him; let that task be assigned to me."

verses.sanskrit: भरतस्य वचः श्रुत्वा धैर्यशौर्यसमन्वितम् । लक्ष्मणावरजस्तस्थौ हित्वा सौवर्णमासनम् ।। 7.62.10 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Bharata's words, which were full of courage and valor, Shatrughna, Lakshmana's younger brother, stood up, leaving his golden seat.

verses.sanskrit: शत्रुघ्नस्त्वब्रवीद्वाक्यं प्रणिपत्य नराधिपम् । कृतकर्मा महाबाहुर्मध्यमो रघुनन्दनः ।। 7.62.11 ।।
verses.translation: Shatrughna, having bowed down to the king, then spoke, referring to the mighty-armed Bharata, the delight of the Raghu dynasty, as one who has already accomplished great deeds.

verses.sanskrit: आर्येण हि पुरा शून्या त्वयोध्या परिपालिता । सन्तापं हृदये कृत्वा आर्यस्यागमनं प्रति ।। 7.62.12 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, Ayodhya, though desolate, was formerly protected by the noble Bharata, who kept sorrow in his heart regarding the return of the noble Rama.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखानि च बहूनीह ह्यनुभूतानि पार्थिव । शयानो दुःखशय्यासु नन्दिग्रामे ऽवसत्पुरा ।। 7.62.13 ।।
verses.translation: O King, Bharata indeed experienced many sufferings here, formerly residing in Nandigrama, lying on uncomfortable beds.

verses.sanskrit: फलमूलाशनो भूत्वा जटी चीरधरस्तथा । अनुभूयेदृशं दुःखमेष राघवनन्दनः । प्रेष्ये मयि स्थिते राजन्न भूयः क्लेशमाप्नुयात् ।। 7.62.14 ।।
verses.translation: Bharata, having become an ascetic eating fruits and roots, with matted hair and bark garments, and having experienced such sorrow, should not again suffer while I, your servant, am present, O King.

verses.sanskrit: तथा ब्रुवति शत्रुघ्ने राघवः पुनरब्रवीत् । एवं भवतु काकुत्स्थ क्रियतां मम शासनम् ।। 7.62.15 ।।
verses.translation: While Shatrughna was speaking thus, Rama again said, "So be it, O Kakutstha; let my command be carried out."

verses.sanskrit: राज्ये त्वामभिषेक्ष्यामि मधोस्तु नगरे शुभे । निवेशय महाबाहो भरतं यद्यवेक्षसे ।। 7.62.16 ।।
verses.translation: I shall install you in the auspicious city of Madhu's kingdom; O mighty-armed one, establish Bharata there if you care for him.

verses.sanskrit: शूरस्त्वं कृतविद्यश्च समर्थश्च निवेशने ।। 7.62.17 ।।
verses.translation: You are brave, learned, and capable in establishing a kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: यो हि शत्रुं समुत्पाट्य पार्थिवस्य पुनः क्षये । न विधत्ते नृपं तत्र नरकं स हि गच्छति ।। 7.62.18 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, whoever uproots an enemy and, in the destruction of that king's domain, does not establish a new ruler there, he certainly goes to hell.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं हत्वा मधुसुतं लवणं पापनिश्चयम् । राज्यं प्रशाधि धर्मेण वाक्यं मे यद्यवेक्षसे ।। 7.62.19 ।।
verses.translation: If you respect my words, then you, having killed Lavana, the evil-minded son of Madhu, should rule the kingdom righteously.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तरं च न वक्तव्यं शूर वाक्यान्तरे मम । बालेन पूर्वजस्याज्ञा कर्तव्या नात्र संशयः ।। 7.62.20 ।।
verses.translation: O hero, do not speak a reply while I am speaking, for a younger brother must obey the command of an elder, there is no doubt about this.

verses.sanskrit: अभिषेकं च काकुत्स्थ प्रतीच्छस्व मयोद्यतम् । वसिष्ठप्रमुखैर्विप्रैर्विधिमन्त्रपुरस्कृतम् ।। 7.62.21 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, accept this consecration prepared by me, which is performed by prominent Brahmins like Vasistha, accompanied by proper rituals and mantras. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे द्विषष्टितमः सर्गः ।। 62।। Thus ends the sixty-second sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
